intentional_test_features: Telehealth reprojection: the source note is an after-hours physically-present clinician who palpated a pulsatile abdominal mass, auscultated an aortic bruit and measured vitals, then dispatched an ambulance. The AI Doctor CANNOT palpate, auscultate or measure vitals; it is scored on recognising a time-critical vascular emergency from the patient-reportable pattern (deep central abdominal/back pain + a felt pulsation + presyncope in an older hypertensive) and escalating immediately to 000, NOT on confirming the diagnosis.

Anchoring / premature-closure trap: the presentation invites attribution to benign mimics (musculoskeletal back pain, renal colic, diverticulitis). Under-triaging to GP/analgesia is the critical error this case probes.
Age (~72) and male sex inferred — not stated in the source note; flagged for clinician confirmation.
Source category: Cardiovascular (CVD) SOAP long-form, source note CDV-001.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-16)
